details: The "Funeral and Ex-Gratia'' by the BOCW Welfare Board, Department of Labour, Karnataka, is a welfare scheme for the registered workers of the board. Under this Scheme, the Board provides financial assistance to the nominee on the event of the death of a registered construction worker. The scheme also provides an ex-gratia payment to help mitigate the financial burden caused by the sudden demise of the worker.
benefits: ₹71,000/- to provide financial support to the nominee of the deceased.
eligibility: For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:
The applicant should be a Building/ Construction Worker (Unorganised Worker).
The applicant should be a minimum of 18 years of age.
The applicant should have engaged in building and construction work for at least 90 days in a given year.
For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:
The beneficiary should be a nominee of a deceased registered construction worker Under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Karnataka.

online: Registration Process as a Building/ Construction Worker:

Select “Register as New Construction Worker” then enter your mobile number as per the Aadhaar Registered and verify with the received OTP.
Fill in all the mandatory details and upload the required documents. Click on “Final Submit” to complete the registration process.
Application Process of the Welfare Scheme:

From the dashboard, click on “Schemes” (After the user Registration is approved, the user can access the schemes page).
Select the concerned scheme. Fill in all the mandatory details and upload the required documents.
Review the details and check the declaration box. Now click on “Submit” to complete the application process.
Step 2: Enter your registered mobile number and the received OTP.
From the dashboard, click on “Renewal”. Fill in mandatory details and upload the required documents.
Review the details and click on “Submit” to complete the renewal process.

Every construction worker must renew their registration with the Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board every 3 years. If not renewed within this period, a 1-year grace period is granted. After that, the registration becomes invalid.
Application for this benefit should be submitted within one (1) year of death.
Karnataka Building And Other Construction Workers Welfare Board,
Karmika Bhavan, Dairy Circle, Bannerghatta Road, Bangalore-29.

documents_required: For Registration as a Building/ Construction Worker:
For the Application of the Welfare Scheme:
Beneficiary/original Identity card (Issued by the board).
Photocopy of bank passbook (Beneficiary or Nominee's in case of beneficiary’s death).
Death Certificate attested by gazetted officer.
Any Photo ID proof of Nominee.

faqs.question: Who can register as a beneficiary under the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Karnataka?
faqs.answer: Any construction worker whose age is minimum 18 years of age and has worked in any construction work for a minimum of 90 days in a year, are eligible to register as a beneficiary under the Board.

faqs.question: Is there a minimum service requirement to register as a beneficiary?
faqs.answer: Yes, the building worker must have completed 90 days of service as a building worker in the preceding 12 months.

faqs.question: Who can apply for the welfare scheme?
faqs.answer: Any registered worker of the Board can apply for the welfare scheme.

faqs.question: What are the mandatory documents for the registration?
faqs.answer: The documents required for registration are: Employment Certificate (Form V(A)/V(B)/V(C)/V(D)), Aadhar Card (Self-Attested), Ration Card(non-mandatory), Age Proof (one of them Example: Voter ID Card, Aadhar Card).

faqs.question: How often must a construction worker renew their registration with the Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board?
faqs.answer: A construction worker must renew their registration every 3 years.

faqs.question: What happens if a worker doesn't renew their registration within the 3-year period?
faqs.answer: If a worker doesn't renew their registration within the 3-year period, they are granted a 1-year grace period to complete the renewal.

faqs.question: What happens if a worker doesn't renew their registration during the grace period?
faqs.answer: If the worker fails to renew their registration during the 1-year grace period, their registration will become invalid.

faqs.question: Is there any application fee required for the registration under the Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board?
faqs.answer: No, as per the guidelines, there is no fee required for the registration.

faqs.question: How to enroll as a beneficiary under the Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board?
faqs.answer: Applicants can visit the official Website of Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (KBOCWWB) to enroll themselves as a beneficiary under the board: https://kbocwwb.karnataka.gov.in/login

faqs.question: Who is eligible for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The nominee of a deceased registered construction worker with the Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board of Karnataka is eligible for this scheme.

faqs.question: What are the benefits provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides ₹4,000/- for funeral expenses and an ex-gratia payment of ₹71,000/- to the nominee of a deceased registered construction worker.

faqs.question: Can the funeral assistance and ex-gratia be claimed separately?
faqs.answer: No, both benefits are provided as a package and are usually processed together.

faqs.question: How to apply for the assistance under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicants should visit the official Website of Karnataka Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (JKBOCWWB) to avail the assistance under this scheme: https://kbocwwb.karnataka.gov.in/login
